# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write Kong configuration file (kong.conf) for production deployment. Set admin_listen to configure Admin API accessibility. Define proxy_listen for client traffic and database connection settings. This is for our Kong Gateway deployment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for kong admin api exposed and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: kong_admin_api_exposed

# Kong Gateway Production Configuration

# ==========================================
# GENERAL SETTINGS
# ==========================================
prefix = /usr/local/kong/
log_level = notice
proxy_access_log = /usr/local/kong/logs/access.log
proxy_error_log = /usr/local/kong/logs/error.log
admin_access_log = /usr/local/kong/logs/admin_access.log
admin_error_log = /usr/local/kong/logs/admin_error.log

# ==========================================
# PROXY LISTENER (Client Traffic)
# ==========================================
proxy_listen = 0.0.0.0:8000, 0.0.0.0:8443 ssl http2
stream_listen = off

# ==========================================
# ADMIN API (SECURED - Internal Only)
# ==========================================
admin_listen = 127.0.0.1:8001, 127.0.0.1:8444 ssl

# Disable Admin API on public interfaces
# Only accessible via localhost or through secure tunnel/VPN
admin_gui_listen = off

# ==========================================
# DATABASE SETTINGS (PostgreSQL)
# ==========================================
database = postgres
pg_host = 127.0.0.1
pg_port = 5432
pg_timeout = 5000
pg_user = kong
pg_password = {vault://env/kong-db-password}
pg_database = kong
pg_ssl = on
pg_ssl_verify = on

# Connection pool settings
pg_max_concurrent_queries = 0
pg_semaphore_timeout = 60000

# ==========================================
# SSL/TLS SETTINGS
# ==========================================
ssl_cert = /usr/local/kong/ssl/kong-default.crt
ssl_cert_key = /usr/local/kong/ssl/kong-default.key
ssl_cipher_suite = intermediate
ssl_protocols = TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3

# Admin API SSL (for internal secure access)
admin_ssl_cert = /usr/local/kong/ssl/admin.crt
admin_ssl_cert_key = /usr/local/kong/ssl/admin.key

# ==========================================
# SECURITY SETTINGS
# ==========================================
trusted_ips = 127.0.0.1,::1
real_ip_header = X-Real-IP
real_ip_recursive = off

# Disable unnecessary features
anonymous_reports = off

# ==========================================
# PERFORMANCE SETTINGS
# ==========================================
nginx_worker_processes = auto
nginx_daemon = off
mem_cache_size = 128m
upstream_keepalive_pool_size = 60
upstream_keepalive_max_requests = 100
upstream_keepalive_idle_timeout = 60

# ==========================================
# DNS RESOLVER
# ==========================================
dns_resolver = 8.8.8.8,8.8.4.4
dns_hostsfile = /etc/hosts
dns_order = LAST,SRV,A,CNAME
dns_valid_ttl = 60
dns_stale_ttl = 3600
dns_not_found_ttl = 30
dns_error_ttl = 1
dns_no_sync = off

# ==========================================
# CLUSTERING (for multi-node deployments)
# ==========================================
cluster_listen = 127.0.0.1:8005
cluster_control_plane = 127.0.0.1:8005
cluster_mtls = shared
cluster_cert = /usr/local/kong/ssl/cluster.crt
cluster_cert_key = /usr/local/kong/ssl/cluster.key
cluster_ca_cert = /usr/local/kong/ssl/cluster-ca.crt

# ==========================================
# ADDITIONAL SECURITY HEADERS
# ==========================================
headers = server_tokens=off, latency_tokens=off
nginx_http_client_body_buffer_size = 8k
nginx_http_client_header_buffer_size = 1k
nginx_http_large_client_header_buffers = 4 8k
client_max_body_size = 10m
client_body_buffer_size = 8k

# ==========================================
# RATE LIMITING & PROTECTION
# ==========================================
nginx_http_lua_shared_dict = kong_rate_limiting_counters 12m
nginx_http_lua_shared_dict = kong_db_cache 128m
nginx_http_lua_shared_dict = kong_db_cache_miss 12m
nginx_http_lua_shared_dict = kong_locks 8m

# ==========================================
# VITALS & MONITORING (Enterprise)
# ==========================================
vitals = on
vitals_strategy = prometheus
vitals_statsd_address = 127.0.0.1:8125
vitals_tsdb_address = 127.0.0.1:9090

# ==========================================
# PORTAL (Enterprise - Disabled by default)
# ==========================================
portal = off
portal_gui_listen = off
portal_api_listen = off

# ==========================================
# RBAC (Enterprise)
# ==========================================
enforce_rbac = on
admin_gui_auth = basic-auth
admin_gui_session_conf = {"secret":"CHANGE_THIS_SECRET","cookie_secure":true,"cookie_httponly":true,"cookie_samesite":"Strict"}

# ==========================================
# AUDIT LOGGING
# ==========================================
audit_log = on
audit_log_ignore_methods = GET,OPTIONS
audit_log_ignore_paths = /status,/health
audit_log_record_ttl = 2592000

# ==========================================
# NOTES FOR PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT
# ==========================================
# 1. Admin API is bound to 127.0.0.1 only - NOT accessible from external networks
# 2. Access Admin API via: SSH tunnel, VPN, or internal network with firewall rules
# 3. Use Kong's RBAC plugin for additional Admin API access control
# 4. Implement IP whitelisting at firewall/load balancer level
# 5. Use strong authentication (basic-auth, OIDC, LDAP) for Admin API
# 6. Rotate secrets and certificates regularly
# 7. Monitor admin_access_log for unauthorized access attempts
# 8. Consider using Kong Manager with proper authentication
# 9. Database credentials should use vault or secrets management
# 10. Enable audit logging for compliance requirements
